workingTitle: LinkedIn acquisition price financing and dilution

decisionWindowBasis.summary: The one-day window is the public board authorization or company announcement date, not an earnings-quarter proxy.
decisionWindowBasis.reviewerNote: The publication date and announcement language were reviewed on the official source.

sourceAssociations.relevanceClaim: The dated official announcement states the concrete terms of LinkedIn acquisition price financing and dilution.

evidenceLocator.exactQuote: Microsoft will acquire LinkedIn for $196 per share in an all-cash transaction valued at $26.2 billion
evidenceLocator.exactRelevancePassage: Microsoft will acquire LinkedIn for $196 per share in an all-cash transaction valued at $26.2 billion

sourceAssociations.reviewerNote: Reviewed against the dated official page and event-specific quoted passage on 2026-07-14.

sourceAssociations.relevanceClaim: The bounded Amy Hood turn identifies the LinkedIn transaction and explains financing timing, growth objectives, and quantified cost synergies using only decision-time information.

evidenceLocator.exactQuote: We will finance this transaction primarily through new debt issued prior to close, which we intend to raise opportunistically during favorable market windows. Our primary objective with the combination is to accelerate our top line growth across LinkedIn’s core business as well as Office 365 and Dynamics. Additionally, we expect at least $150 million annually in cost synergies as we naturally combine two public company entities.
evidenceLocator.exactRelevancePassage: Under the terms of the definitive agreement, we’ve agreed to acquire LinkedIn for $196 per share in an all-cash transaction that is approximately $26 billion inclusive of LinkedIn’s net cash. The transaction has been unanimously approved by the boards of directors of both Microsoft and LinkedIn. And Reid Hoffman, the chairman, co-founder, and controlling shareholder of LinkedIn, has stated he will vote all his shares in accordance with their board’s recommendation. We expect the transaction to close by the end of the calendar year during the second quarter of our Fiscal ’17 subject to approval by LinkedIn shareholders, and the satisfaction of certain regulatory approvals and other customary closing conditions. We will finance this transaction primarily through new debt issued prior to close, which we intend to raise opportunistically during favorable market windows. Our primary objective with the combination is to accelerate our top line growth across LinkedIn’s core business as well as Office 365 and Dynamics. Additionally, we expect at least $150 million annually in cost synergies as we naturally combine two public company entities.
evidenceLocator.speaker: Amy Hood

sourceAssociations.reviewerNote: review:review-linkedin-direct-2026-07-15-v1 The bounded Amy Hood turn identifies the LinkedIn transaction and explains financing timing, growth objectives, and quantified cost synergies using only decision-time information.

workingTitle: Nuance healthcare cloud acquisition for 19.7 billion dollars

decisionWindowBasis.summary: The one-day window is the public board authorization or company announcement date, not an earnings-quarter proxy.
decisionWindowBasis.reviewerNote: The publication date and announcement language were reviewed on the official source.

sourceAssociations.relevanceClaim: The dated official announcement states the concrete terms of Nuance healthcare cloud acquisition for 19.7 billion dollars.

evidenceLocator.exactQuote: Microsoft will acquire Nuance for $56.00 per share
evidenceLocator.exactRelevancePassage: Microsoft will acquire Nuance for $56.00 per share, implying a 23% premium to the closing price of Nuance on Friday, April 9, in an all-cash transaction valued at $19.7 billion

sourceAssociations.reviewerNote: Reviewed against the dated official page and event-specific quoted passage on 2026-07-14.

sourceAssociations.relevanceClaim: Discovery metadata from PDF pages [9, 10]; original artifact review is required before evidence approval. Live recheck: HTTP 400; unavailable.
sourceAssociations.reviewerNote: review_required: Discovery metadata from PDF pages [9, 10]; original artifact review is required before evidence approval. Live recheck: HTTP 400; unavailable.

sourceAssociations.relevanceClaim: Amy Hood states Microsoft's M&A selection criteria and applies them to the Nuance acquisition terms, price, execution requirements, and dilution guardrails.

evidenceLocator.exactQuote: We focus on TAM-expansive opportunities in high-growth markets, where we can uniquely add value to the users or the community.
evidenceLocator.exactRelevancePassage: Over the past 10 years, we've taken a consistent approach to mergers and acquisitions of all sizes. We focus on TAM-expansive opportunities in high-growth markets, where we can uniquely add value to the users or the community. And we execute those transactions at a price that supports long-term shareholder value creation. As Satya, Mark and Scott discussed, Nuance meets those criteria. And with all acquisitions, value was ultimately only created with terrific execution, bind aligned team with a shared vision of the future. For this, key indicators of our success will be accelerated top line growth of Nuance business starting with Microsoft Cloud for Healthcare, increased adoption of Azure, Teams and Dynamics 365 across industries. Now let's turn to the transaction details. We've agreed to acquire Nuance for $56 per share, implying a 23% premium to Nuance's closing price on April 9, in an all-cash transaction valued at $19.7 billion.
evidenceLocator.speaker: Amy Hood

sourceAssociations.reviewerNote: Codex reviewed the speaker-separated acquisition-call transcript, exact Amy Hood passage, and official Microsoft/SEC transaction corroboration on 2026-07-21.

evidenceLocator.exactRelevancePassage: Microsoft Corp (Nasdaq: MSFT) and Nuance Communications, Inc. workingTitle: Activision Blizzard acquisition for 68.7 billion dollars

decisionWindowBasis.summary: The one-day window is the public board authorization or company announcement date, not an earnings-quarter proxy.
decisionWindowBasis.reviewerNote: The publication date and announcement language were reviewed on the official source.

sourceAssociations.relevanceClaim: The dated official announcement states the concrete terms of Activision Blizzard acquisition for 68.7 billion dollars.

evidenceLocator.exactQuote: Microsoft will acquire Activision Blizzard for $95.00 per share
evidenceLocator.exactRelevancePassage: Microsoft will acquire Activision Blizzard for $95.00 per share, in an all-cash transaction valued at $68.7 billion

sourceAssociations.reviewerNote: Reviewed against the dated official page and event-specific quoted passage on 2026-07-14.

sourceAssociations.relevanceClaim: The bounded Amy Hood turn identifies the Activision Blizzard transaction, price, growth mechanisms, monetization outcomes, and expected EPS accretion using decision-time information.

evidenceLocator.exactQuote: Key measures of our success include accelerated revenue growth from Activision Blizzard’s game portfolio as we extend content to more devices, resulting in increased engagement and monetization across the Xbox platform, as well as additional growth in Game Pass subscribers as we attract new players wherever they play and continue to build one of the most compelling and diverse lineups of triple-A content available.
evidenceLocator.exactRelevancePassage: Key measures of our success include accelerated revenue growth from Activision Blizzard’s game portfolio as we extend content to more devices, resulting in increased engagement and monetization across the Xbox platform, as well as additional growth in Game Pass subscribers as we attract new players wherever they play and continue to build one of the most compelling and diverse lineups of triple-A content available. Now, let’s turn to the transaction details. We’ve agreed to acquire Activision Blizzard for $95 per share in an all-cash transaction valued at $68.7 billion, inclusive of Activision Blizzard’s net cash. The acquisition will not impact our previously announced share buy-back program. The transaction has been approved by the board of directors of both Microsoft and Activision Blizzard. The transaction is expected to close in fiscal year 2023 and is subject to approval by Activision Blizzard shareholders and the satisfaction of certain regulatory approvals and other customary closing conditions. On a non-gap basis, we anticipate the transaction will be accretive to EPS upon close.
evidenceLocator.speaker: Amy Hood

sourceAssociations.reviewerNote: review:review-activision-direct-2026-07-15-v1 The bounded Amy Hood turn identifies the Activision Blizzard transaction, price, growth mechanisms, monetization outcomes, and expected EPS accretion using decision-time information.
